title: E2E happy path covers add, edit, and delete employee features
test_data: Generate one unique employee record for the full flow.
gherkin_steps:
Given I am on the Paylocity login page
When I login with configured credentials
Then I should see the benefits dashboard
When I add a new employee through the UI modal
Then the new employee should be visible in the employee table
And payroll columns Gross Pay, Benefits Cost, and Net Pay should match expected business-rule values for that employee
And the dashboard should remain in a valid state
When I open the edit modal for the created employee
Then the edit modal should show the created employee values
When I edit the existing employee through the UI modal
Then the employee row should show updated values
And payroll columns Gross Pay, Benefits Cost, and Net Pay should match expected business-rule values for that employee
And the dashboard should remain in a valid state
When I delete the existing employee and confirm
Then the employee should not be visible in the table by id
And the table should show no employees message
And the dashboard should remain in a valid state
expected_result: The main happy path flow succeeds end to end for add, edit, and delete features, including payroll calculations and final empty state validation.
notes: Smoke should stay simple and fast. Covers the primary user journey only.

title: E2E happy path covers create, read, update, and delete employee operations
test_data: Examples: create POST /api/Employees (200), read GET /api/Employees/{id} (200), update PUT /api/Employees (200), delete DELETE /api/Employees/{id} (200/204).
gherkin_steps:
Given API smoke prerequisites are ready for "<operation>"
When I execute API smoke operation "<operation>"
Then API smoke operation "<operation>" should return "<expectedStatus>"
And API smoke payroll should follow business rules for "<operation>"
And I attach API smoke trace for operation "<operation>" using "<method>" on "<endpoint>"
expected_result: Each CRUD smoke operation returns the expected status; payroll business rules are validated for create/read/update; request/response trace is attached.
notes: Smoke should validate the primary API journey quickly without replacing detailed negative coverage.

title: Dashboard loads and employee table is visible
gherkin_steps:
Given I am authenticated and open the Benefits Dashboard
When the page finishes loading
Then the employees table should be visible
And the Add Employee button should be visible
And the page should be usable without console-blocking errors
expected_result: Dashboard renders successfully with main controls available.
notes: Moved to detailed/validation coverage after introducing E2E smoke flow.

title: Add employee from UI with valid data
test_data: Generate unique firstName, lastName, username-compatible seed, dependants=1
gherkin_steps:
Given I am authenticated on the benefits dashboard
When I add a new employee through the UI modal
Then the new employee should be visible in the employee table
And payroll columns Gross Pay, Benefits Cost, and Net Pay should match expected business-rule values for that employee
expected_result: New employee is created and displayed with correct calculated payroll values.
notes: Cleanup should remove created employee at test end.

title: Edit employee from UI with valid data
preconditions: Create prerequisite employee by API hook before opening the dashboard.
test_data: Employee seeded by API; update dependants and/or names with unique values
gherkin_steps:
Given I am authenticated on the benefits dashboard
When I edit the existing employee through the UI modal
Then the employee row should show updated values
And payroll columns Gross Pay, Benefits Cost, and Net Pay should match expected business-rule values for that employee
expected_result: Employee data is updated and visible in the table with correct recalculated payroll values.
notes: Preferred setup via API hook to keep test focused on edit.

title: Delete a single employee from UI
preconditions: Create prerequisite employee by API hook before opening the dashboard.
test_data: Employee seeded by API
gherkin_steps:
Given an employee exists in the system
And I am on the Benefits Dashboard
When I open Delete for that employee
And I confirm the deletion
Then the employee should be removed from the table
expected_result: Employee record is deleted and no longer shown in the table.
notes: Simple delete validation and good smoke component. Delete itself acts as cleanup.

title: Cancel delete does not remove employee
preconditions: Create prerequisite employee by API hook.
test_data: Employee seeded by API
gherkin_steps:
Given an employee exists in the system
When I open Delete for that employee
And I cancel the delete modal
Then the employee should remain in the table
expected_result: Employee is not deleted when delete is cancelled.
notes: Cleanup should delete employee after validation.

title: Cancel add or edit does not persist changes
preconditions: For edit path, create prerequisite employee by API hook.
test_data: Unique values for attempted add/edit
gherkin_steps:
Given I am on the Benefits Dashboard
When I open Add Employee or Edit Employee
And I enter modified data
And I cancel the modal
Then no new employee should be created
And no existing employee should be changed
expected_result: Cancel dismisses modal without persisting changes.
notes: Can be split into separate tests later if desired.

title: Add vs Update buttons toggle correctly in employee modal
preconditions: Create prerequisite employee by API hook for edit path.
gherkin_steps:
Given I am on the Benefits Dashboard
When I open Add Employee
Then the Add button should be visible
And the Update button should be hidden
When I open Edit for an existing employee
Then the Update button should be visible
And the Add button should be hidden
expected_result: Modal shows the correct action button for the current mode.
notes: Based on current UI behavior.

title: Add Employee modal opens with empty input fields
gherkin_steps:
Given I previously interacted with employee data in the UI
When I open Add Employee
Then the id field should be empty
And first name should be empty
And last name should be empty
And dependants should be empty
expected_result: The Add Employee modal opens in a clean state with blank inputs for a new employee.
notes: Important to avoid stale data being carried over from a previous add or edit action.

title: Empty state is shown when no employees exist
preconditions: Environment or test setup must ensure no employee records are present for the tenant under test.
gherkin_steps:
Given there are no employees in the system
When I open the Benefits Dashboard
Then the table should show the empty-state message
expected_result: No employees found message is displayed.
notes: May need controlled environment; can be fragile in shared envs or add a clean-up api hook to remove all employees first.

title: Displayed calculations are correct for a known employee record
preconditions: Create employee by API hook with controlled dependants count.
test_data: Dataset by dependants count, e.g. 0, 1, 2, 32
gherkin_steps:
Given an employee exists with a known dependants value
When I open the Benefits Dashboard
Then payroll columns Gross Pay, Benefits Cost, and Net Pay should match expected business-rule values for that employee
expected_result: Calculated values match expected payroll and benefits calculations.
notes: Best implemented as data-driven by dependants count.

title: Table headers map to the correct employee values
preconditions: Create employee with clearly distinguishable first and last names by API hook.
test_data: Example: firstName='Alpha', lastName='Zulu'
gherkin_steps:
Given an employee exists with distinct first and last names
When I open the Benefits Dashboard
Then the value under First Name should be the employee first name
And the value under Last Name should be the employee last name
expected_result: Column labels and data mapping are correct.
notes: Important to detect potential UI header-to-cell mapping defects.

title: Add employee modal handles invalid dependants without broken submit flow or persisted data
test_data: Dataset: negative dependants, above-max dependants, decimal dependants, text dependants, blank dependants, and spaces-only dependants.
gherkin_steps:
Given I am on the Benefits Dashboard
When I open Add Employee
And I enter valid employee data except for an invalid dependants value
And I submit the form
Then the employee should not be created
And the modal should complete a clear validation outcome instead of getting stuck
And no new employee should appear in the table
expected_result: Invalid dependants values must be handled without persisting a new employee and without leaving the submit flow in a broken or stuck modal state.
notes: Automated assertions should verify failed creation, no persisted record, and that the modal does not remain stuck after save is pressed.

title: UI validation for missing required employee fields
test_data: Dataset: missing firstName, missing lastName, all visible fields empty including dependants blank
gherkin_steps:
Given I am on the Benefits Dashboard
When I attempt to save an employee with missing required data
Then the system should block save or present a failure consistently
expected_result: Required field handling is consistent and does not create invalid records. For the all-fields-empty case, if the modal stays open and the submit flow times out, that should be treated as a test failure signal rather than a successful save.
notes: Use dataset variations for blank or partially completed employee records. Include observation that dependants blank currently may leave the UI without visible feedback.

title: Table refreshes after add, edit, and delete actions
preconditions: Create prerequisite employee for edit/delete portions via API hook as needed.
test_data: Unique employee data
gherkin_steps:
Given I perform add, edit, or delete through the UI
When each action completes successfully
Then the table should refresh and show the latest state
expected_result: Table reflects the latest record state after each CRUD action.
notes: Can become 3 separate tests later.

title: Edit modal shows the selected employee data correctly
preconditions: Create prerequisite employee by API hook before opening the dashboard.
test_data: Employee seeded by API with known firstName, lastName, and dependants values.
gherkin_steps:
Given an employee exists in the system
And I am on the Benefits Dashboard
When I open Edit for that employee
Then the modal should show the correct id
And the first name should match the selected employee
And the last name should match the selected employee
And the dependants value should match the selected employee
expected_result: Edit opens with the exact data for the selected employee and is ready for update.
notes: Important because the same modal is reused and row-to-column mapping may be incorrect.

title: Edit modal rejects invalid dependants values and does not save the changes
preconditions: Create prerequisite employee by API hook before opening the dashboard.
test_data: Dataset: negative dependants, above-max dependants, decimal dependants, text dependants, blank dependants, and spaces-only dependants.
gherkin_steps:
Given an employee exists in the system
And I am on the Benefits Dashboard
When I open Edit for that employee
And I replace dependants with an invalid value
And I submit the form
Then the employee should not be updated with the invalid value
And the modal should not complete as a successful save
And the previously persisted employee data should remain unchanged
expected_result: Invalid dependants values are rejected during edit and no invalid update is saved. Blank or negative dependants may manifest as a stuck modal or submit timeout instead of a clean inline validation.
notes: Automated assertions should verify failed update and unchanged persisted data. For blank or negative dependants, treat a blocked save or modal timeout as a valid failure signal and log it as a UI issue.

title: UI shows visible feedback when save fails due to invalid data
preconditions: Valid session. Use invalid create or edit datasets that the API rejects.
test_data: Dataset may reuse invalid dependants values and other invalid form combinations.
gherkin_steps:
Given I am on the Benefits Dashboard
When I try to save employee data that the API rejects
Then the UI should show a visible and understandable error message
And the user should not be left with a silent failure state
expected_result: Users receive clear feedback when save fails instead of seeing no action and no explanation.
notes: Hybrid because the presence of an error container can be automated, but message clarity and UX quality benefit from human review.

title: Delete flow remains correct when multiple employees are present
preconditions: Create multiple prerequisite employees by API hook before opening the dashboard.
test_data: At least two or three seeded employees with distinct names for easy identification.
gherkin_steps:
Given multiple employees exist in the system
And I am on the Benefits Dashboard
When I delete one selected employee from the table
Then only the selected employee should be removed
And the remaining employees should still be present
And the table should remain consistent after the deletion
expected_result: Delete works correctly in a realistic multi-record scenario without affecting other rows.
notes: More complete delete coverage than the single-record smoke path.

title: Payroll calculations are correct for accepted dependants limits
preconditions: Valid login/session is available and employee can be seeded by API hook.
test_data: Accepted dependants dataset: 0, 1, 31, 32
gherkin_steps:
Given I am authenticated on the benefits dashboard
And an employee exists with dependants <dependants> via API
Then payroll columns Gross Pay, Benefits Cost, and Net Pay should match expected business-rule values for that employee
expected_result: For accepted dependants limits, displayed payroll columns match expected business-rule values.
notes: Focuses on valid boundary values where calculation must be correct.

title: Add employee modal handles invalid firstName or lastName values without broken submit flow or persisted data
test_data: Dataset: numeric-only names, blank names, spaces-only names, selected mixed alphanumeric names, and names over 50 characters.
gherkin_steps:
Given I am on the Benefits Dashboard
When I open Add Employee
And I enter employee data using an invalid firstName or lastName variation
And I submit the form
Then the employee should not be created and the submit flow should not break
And invalid name values should not appear as accepted visible employee names in the table
And firstName and lastName values should not appear under the wrong headers
expected_result: Invalid name values such as numeric-only, blank, spaces-only, or longer-than-50-character entries must be handled without persisting bad data or leaving the modal in a broken submit state. Visible table mapping must still keep firstName under First Name and lastName under Last Name.
notes: Use readable dataset names in automation output instead of raw numeric strings so failures are easier to understand.

title: Add employee modal accepts valid boundary values and persists them correctly
test_data: Dataset: minimum valid names with minimum dependants, maximum valid names with maximum dependants, and representative valid edge combinations.
gherkin_steps:
Given I am on the Benefits Dashboard
When I open Add Employee
And I enter employee data using a valid boundary variation
And I submit the form
Then the employee should be created successfully
And firstName and lastName should be visible under the correct headers
And dependants should be saved with the expected boundary value
And payroll columns Gross Pay, Benefits Cost, and Net Pay should match expected business-rule values for that employee
expected_result: Valid boundary inputs in the add modal are accepted and persisted correctly without swapping name columns or miscomputing payroll.
notes: Use readable dataset names in automation output instead of raw generated values so edge-case failures are easy to understand.

title: Create employee with valid payload
test_data: Unique payload with valid firstName, lastName, username, dependants
gherkin_steps:
Given I have a valid employee payload
When I create the employee via API
Then the employee is created successfully
And the created employee payroll should match business rules
expected_result: API creates employee successfully and returns payroll values matching business rules.
notes: Cleanup should delete created employee.

title: Get all employees returns a valid list
gherkin_steps:
Given a valid Authorization header
When I send a GET request for all employees
Then the API should return a successful response
And the response body should be a list of employees
expected_result: Employee list endpoint is reachable and returns the expected collection shape.
notes: Detailed functional coverage after introducing API E2E smoke flow.

title: Get employee by valid id
preconditions: Create employee by API as test data setup.
test_data: Seeded employee id
gherkin_steps:
Given an employee exists via API
When I request employee by id
Then the employee by id response should match seeded employee
And the employee by id payroll should match business rules
expected_result: GET by id returns the correct employee and payroll values matching business rules.
notes: Cleanup should delete employee after test.

title: Update employee with valid payload
preconditions: Create employee by API as test data setup.
test_data: Seeded employee id plus updated valid values
gherkin_steps:
Given an employee exists via API
When I update the seeded employee with valid data
Then updated employee data should be persisted
And updated employee payroll should match business rules
expected_result: API updates employee and persists new values with correct recalculated payroll fields.
notes: Cleanup should delete employee after test.

title: Delete employee by valid id
preconditions: Create employee by API as test data setup.
test_data: Seeded employee id
gherkin_steps:
Given an employee exists
When I send a DELETE request using that employee id
Then the employee should be deleted successfully
And a follow-up read should confirm the record is no longer available
expected_result: Employee is deleted and cannot be retrieved afterward.
notes: Delete is the cleanup.

title: Required field validation on create/update
test_data: Dataset: missing firstName, missing lastName, missing username, missing multiple required fields
gherkin_steps:
Given a valid Authorization header
When I submit a create or update request with a required-field dataset variation
Then the API should reject invalid payloads consistently
expected_result: Missing required fields are not accepted.
notes: Use required-field datasets instead of duplicating separate missing-field tests.

title: String length boundary validation for firstName lastName and username
test_data: Dataset lengths: 0, 1, 49, 50, 51 for each field
gherkin_steps:
Given a valid Authorization header
When I submit payloads using boundary-length dataset variations
Then the API should accept valid lengths and reject invalid lengths consistently
expected_result: Field length constraints are enforced correctly.
notes: Strong data-driven candidate.

title: Dependants boundary validation
gherkin_steps:
Given a valid Authorization header
When I submit employee payloads with dependants boundary dataset values
Then the API should accept values within range and reject values outside range consistently
expected_result: Dependants range validation behaves as documented.
notes: Use boundary datasets for dependants values instead of creating separate near-limit tests.

title: Invalid UUID handling for get and delete by id
test_data: Dataset: malformed uuid, empty id, non-existent but valid uuid
gherkin_steps:
Given a valid Authorization header
When I request get or delete using an invalid id dataset variation
Then the API should fail gracefully and consistently
expected_result: Invalid id requests are handled predictably without incorrect success.
notes: Can also cover not-found behavior separately if needed.

title: Read-only fields cannot be client-controlled
test_data: Payloads attempting to set partitionKey, sortKey, gross, benefitsCost, net
gherkin_steps:
Given a valid Authorization header
When I submit payloads that attempt to control read-only fields
Then the API should ignore or reject those values consistently
expected_result: Read-only fields are not writable by the client.
notes: Important contract integrity check.

title: Unexpected properties are rejected when additionalProperties is false
test_data: Payload with extra property such as middleName or unknownFlag
gherkin_steps:
Given a valid Authorization header
When I submit a payload with an undefined property
Then the API should reject the payload consistently
expected_result: Unexpected fields are not silently accepted.
notes: Validates strict schema behavior.

title: Expiration field rejects invalid date-time format
test_data: Dataset: valid ISO date-time, invalid text, invalid date, empty string
gherkin_steps:
Given a valid Authorization header
When I submit payloads with expiration dataset variations
Then the API should accept valid date-time values and reject invalid ones consistently
expected_result: Expiration field format is validated correctly.
notes: Only applicable if expiration is supported in requests for this tenant/implementation.

title: Authentication is required for employee endpoints
test_data: Dataset: no Authorization header, malformed header, invalid token
gherkin_steps:
Given I call an employee endpoint without valid authentication
When I submit the request
Then the API should reject the request
expected_result: Unauthorized access is not allowed.
notes: Apply to representative endpoints if not all.

title: End-to-end CRUD chain remains consistent
test_data: Unique valid payload
gherkin_steps:
Given a valid Authorization header
When I create an employee
And I retrieve the employee
And I update the employee
And I retrieve the employee again
And I delete the employee
Then each operation should reflect the correct current state
expected_result: CRUD chain is internally consistent across API operations.
notes: Strong regression anchor.

title: API rejects invalid firstName or lastName values or exposes them as defects if accepted
test_data: Dataset: numeric-only names, blank names, spaces-only names, selected mixed alphanumeric names, and names over 50 characters.
gherkin_steps:
Given a valid Authorization header
When I submit employee payloads using invalid name variations
Then the API should reject invalid name values consistently or the test should flag accepted invalid names as defects
And firstName and lastName should remain mapped to the correct response properties
expected_result: Invalid name values such as numeric-only, blank, spaces-only, or longer-than-50-character entries are rejected or clearly detected as defects if the API accepts them. Response mapping must keep firstName and lastName in the correct properties.
notes: Use readable dataset names in automation output instead of raw numeric strings so failures are easier to understand.

title: Get employees endpoint responds within acceptable threshold
gherkin_steps:
Given a valid Authorization header
When I request the employee list
Then the response time should remain within the agreed smoke threshold
expected_result: Endpoint performance is acceptable for smoke validation.
notes: Threshold can be set by project convention.

title: Create update and delete operations respond within acceptable threshold
test_data: Unique valid payload
gherkin_steps:
Given valid employee payload data
When I create update and delete an employee through the API
Then each operation should complete within the agreed smoke threshold
expected_result: CRUD endpoints meet basic performance expectations.
notes: Can capture timings per operation.

title: Repeated CRUD operations remain stable
test_data: Iteration counts such as 5, 10, 20
gherkin_steps:
Given valid employee payload data
When I repeat CRUD operations for a defined iteration dataset
Then the API should remain stable without unexpected failures
expected_result: Basic repeated usage does not introduce instability.
notes: Keep challenge scope lightweight.

title: Add employee action is reachable by keyboard
gherkin_steps:
Given I am authenticated on the benefits dashboard
When I reset keyboard focus to the dashboard
Then the add employee action should be reachable by keyboard
expected_result: Add action can be reached using keyboard-only navigation.
notes: Automated keyboard path check.

title: Edit action for seeded employee is reachable by keyboard
test_data: Seed one employee by API hook
gherkin_steps:
Given I am authenticated on the benefits dashboard
When I reset keyboard focus to the dashboard
Then the edit action for the seeded employee should be reachable by keyboard
expected_result: Edit action for an existing row can be reached using keyboard-only navigation.
notes: Automated keyboard path check with seeded row prerequisite.

title: Delete action for seeded employee is reachable by keyboard
test_data: Seed one employee by API hook
gherkin_steps:
Given I am authenticated on the benefits dashboard
When I reset keyboard focus to the dashboard
Then the delete action for the seeded employee should be reachable by keyboard
expected_result: Delete action for an existing row can be reached using keyboard-only navigation.
notes: Automated keyboard path check with seeded row prerequisite.

title: Employee add modal accessibility happy path
gherkin_steps:
Given I am authenticated on the benefits dashboard
When I open add employee modal from dashboard
Then add and cancel buttons should be visible in the employee modal
And keyboard navigation should reach employee modal inputs and action buttons
And employee modal inputs should have non-empty placeholders
expected_result: Add modal accessibility checks validate modal actions, keyboard reachability, and placeholder presence without extending into employee creation flow.
notes: Focused on modal accessibility behavior only so failures stop at the relevant UI state.

title: Labels buttons and icon actions are understandable
gherkin_steps:
Given I review the dashboard and employee table
When I inspect labels, buttons, and icon-only actions
Then controls should be understandable and not rely on ambiguous visual cues
expected_result: Users can identify actions without confusion.
notes: Manual because clarity and ambiguity require human judgment.

title: Employee table remains usable on practical viewport sizes
preconditions: Dashboard contains at least one record with normal-length values.
gherkin_steps:
Given I open the Benefits Dashboard at different viewport sizes
When I review the employee table and actions
Then the table should remain readable and usable without severe layout breakage
expected_result: Table layout remains practical across targeted viewport sizes.
notes: Manual because visual fit and usability are best judged by a human.

title: Long values do not cause severe overflow or truncation issues
preconditions: Create employee data with long names and realistic ids.
test_data: Dataset with long first/last names and normal/long visible values
gherkin_steps:
Given the table contains records with long visible values
When I review the dashboard layout
Then content should not create severe clipping overlap or unusable truncation
expected_result: Long values do not break layout beyond acceptable limits.
notes: Manual visual check.

title: Modal remains readable and usable under zoom
gherkin_steps:
Given I open the employee or delete modal
When I review it at different zoom levels
Then labels fields and actions should remain readable and usable
expected_result: Modal remains practical under zoom changes.
notes: Manual because readability and usability need human review.
